message.content:
You are the UI/UX reviewer for the Marketify project (an Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from companies).

This is the **third review pass** on the design spec. You previously approved it "with follow-ups" after the second pass. All 10 follow-ups you raised (3 must-fix + 7 nice-to-haves) have been applied, and a subsequent cleanup pass stripped Q-tagged historical framing, version numbers, and the "resolved questions" appendix so the docs read as forward-looking specs.

**Your task:** Read all three finalized documents end-to-end and give a final verdict. Be critical — this is the last review before development starts.

Documents to review:
1. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/product-plan.md` — product/business spec
2. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/design.md` — UI/UX spec (your primary focus)
3. `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/docs/tech-architecture.md` — backend/schema spec (cross-check for design↔schema disagreements)

**Focus areas for your lens:**
- Design consistency and coverage (is every screen/state specified?)
- Interaction states (loading, empty, error, stale, offline, rate-limited)
- Accessibility (contrast, Dynamic Type, reduced-motion, focus order, screen reader)
- Motion discipline (spring presets, stagger skip, celebration anchors)
- Deep-link & back-stack coherence
- Copy tone consistency
- Design↔schema agreement (component props match schema columns)
- Edge cases the spec doesn't cover

**Output format:**
- **Verdict:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT | APPROVED WITH MINOR FOLLOW-UPS | BLOCKED
- **Must-fix before dev:** numbered list (empty is fine if nothing)
- **Nice-to-haves:** numbered list (defer-ok items)
- **What's strong:** 2-4 bullets on what the spec does well
- **Final take:** 2-3 sentences — is this ready to build?

Keep the report focused. Don't re-raise issues from previous passes that have been addressed; verify they're resolved instead. Under 800 words total.

1	# Marketify — Product Plan
2	
3	*The canonical product spec. Synthesizes the initial PRD with the design (`design.md`) and technical (`tech-architecture.md`) plans, which hold authoritative detail.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 0. TL;DR
8	
9	A mobile marketplace (Expo + React Native + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from companies.
10	
11	- **Two roles**, chosen at onboarding (no dual-role accounts in v1).
12	- **Creators** sign up with a username + at least one social handle (TikTok and/or Instagram). **No OAuth** — we pull public profile data via **Apify scrapers** (`clockworks/tiktok-scraper` and `apify/instagram-scraper`). The app trusts whatever handle the creator types; handle ownership is unverified in v1.
13	- **Listers** sign up with username + email + org name.
14	- Listers post campaigns with **pre-conditions** (follower/view thresholds) and **post-conditions** (content rules).
15	- Creators see only eligible campaigns by default, apply, get approved, film, submit a video URL, get reviewed.
16	
17	**Design language:** Disciplined Neubrutalism — hard shadows, 2px ink borders, pink/orange creator-economy palette, Reanimated-driven micro-interactions.
18	
19	**No payments in v1.** `price_cents` is display-only; the creator–lister payment handshake happens off-platform.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 1. The Core User Loop
24	
25	```
26	CREATOR                                   LISTER
27	────────                                  ──────
28	Sign up with username + handle(s)         Sign up with username + email
29	        │                                         │
30	(Apify scrapes profile                            ↓
31	 in background ~30s)                      Create campaign (5-step wizard)
32	        │                                         │
33	        ↓                                         ↓
34	Browse feed ←─────────────────── Publish → status=active
35	   │  (filter: eligible only — default)
36	   ↓
37	Tap campaign → eligibility evaluated live
38	   │
39	   ↓ (if eligible)
40	Apply (optional pitch) ─────────────────► Lister inbox (Realtime)
41	                                             │
42	                                             ↓
43	                              Approve / Reject application
44	   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
45	   │
46	   ↓ (if approved)
47	Film → paste video URL ─────────────────► Lister review (embedded video + post-condition checklist)
48	                                             │
49	                                             ↓
50	                              Approve / Reject submission
51	   (Realtime push to creator) ◄──────────────┘
52	```
53	
54	Two cross-cutting mechanics the PRD surfaced:
55	
56	- **Listing versioning + auto-cancel.** Editing price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or `max_submissions` bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to all pending applications, with notifications.
57	- **Metrics refresh.** Creators can't edit their numbers. Pulled via Apify on sign-up, refreshed daily by cron, or manually pulled once per 6 hours per platform via pull-to-refresh.
58	
59	---
60	
61	## 2. Identity Model — Apify, not OAuth
62	
63	The PRD's original ask was *"no auth — users just select a role and create an account with a username and social media links."* TikTok and Instagram APIs both require OAuth (TikTok's `user.info.stats` scope; IG's Business/Creator Graph API token) to expose follower and view counts — unacceptable friction for a consumer app. Our answer: skip the platform APIs entirely and scrape public profile pages via Apify.
64	
65	- **Creators** sign up with: username + at least one of `tiktok_handle`, `instagram_handle`. No password, no platform consent, no Business/Creator account conversion, no server-side OAuth token management.
66	- A custom JWT signed by our edge function is the session. No Supabase Auth.
67	- At signup, we kick up to three Apify runs (TikTok profile, IG details, IG posts) with a 60s `waitSecs` — the common case is that metrics populate before the user finishes the next screen.
68	- **Trust model:** handles are *unverified.* A creator could type a handle they don't own, and we would scrape and display that account's metrics against their profile. In v1 we accept this risk because (a) it doesn't give them money — `price` is display-only — and (b) listers re-check eligibility at approval time and see the actual submitted video's author before approving. Verification can ship in v2 via a TikTok/IG "post this code in your bio" challenge if abuse emerges.
69	
70	**Trade-off with listers:** listers trust the displayed metrics when reviewing applications. If a creator spoofs a popular handle, they pass the numeric pre-check but the lister still judges the *submitted video* — the fraud is self-limiting. We surface handles in monospace (`@xxx`) so listers can tap through to verify before approving.
71	
72	---
73	
74	## 3. Scope — What Ships in v1
75	
76	### 3.1 IN (build this first)
77	
78	**Foundations**
79	- Supabase project + full schema (`tech-architecture.md` §4.7)
80	- Custom JWT auth signed by our edge function (RLS-aware)
81	- Design tokens + base component library (Neubrutalism primitives)
82	- Apify client wrapper + webhook receiver edge function
83	
84	**Creator**
85	- Handle-based signup (username + TikTok and/or Instagram handle)
86	- Initial Apify scrape on sign-up (synchronous `waitSecs: 60`, falls back to webhook completion)
87	- 6h-throttled manual metrics refresh + daily cron
88	- Campaign feed with client-side eligibility filter (`Eligible only` toggle, default on)
89	- Campaign detail with live eligibility evaluation
90	- Apply flow with server-side eligibility re-check
91	- My Applications (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled)
92	- Submission composer (paste URL → oEmbed validate → self-affirm post-conditions)
93	- My Submissions (pending / approved / rejected)
94	- Profile (linked handles, metrics, pull-to-refresh, add/update/unlink)
95	- In-app notifications inbox
96	
97	**Lister**
98	- Username/email signup
99	- 5-step create-campaign wizard
100	- My Campaigns (active / inactive)
101	- Edit campaign with cascade-cancel confirmation (*"This will cancel N applications"*)
102	- Applications inbox (with creator metrics surfaced)
103	- Application review (approve with optional re-eligibility override, reject)
104	- Submissions inbox
105	- Submission review (embedded video + post-condition checklist + feedback)
106	- In-app notifications inbox
107	
108	**Cross-cutting**
109	- Listing versioning + cascade trigger (Postgres trigger, §4.7)
110	- Metrics staleness marker cron (hourly; surfaces "Outdated" chip after 24h)
111	- Realtime subscriptions (notifications, applications, submissions, metric_snapshots)
112	- Append-only `events` audit table
113	
114	### 3.2 OUT — explicitly cut from v1
115	
116	| # | Cut | Why | Revisit |
117	|---|---|---|---|
118	| 1 | Payment / escrow / Stripe | `price` is display-only; off-platform handshake | v2 |
119	| 2 | Post-submission performance tracking (view count, ROI) | Requires repeated Apify runs per submission | v2 as "Marketify Analytics" |
120	| 3 | Push notifications | APNs/FCM provisioning friction | v1.1 |
121	| 4 | Revision-request flow on submissions | Reduces state-machine complexity; column reserved | v1.1 |
122	| 5 | Handle ownership verification ("post this code in your bio") | Accept spoofing risk in v1; self-limiting via submission review | v2 if abuse emerges |
123	| 6 | In-app admin moderation UI | Reports logged to table, admin via Supabase Studio | v2 |
124	| 7 | Lister analytics dashboard | Just counts in v1 | v2 |
125	| 8 | Creator/lister blocklists | Report-only | v2 |
126	| 9 | In-app chat | Out of scope | v2 |
127	| 10 | Auto-close-on-max-submissions | Listers close manually; re-add when usage clarifies | v1.1 |
128	| 11 | Sample-video upload to Storage | External URLs only (TikTok/IG links) | v2 |
129	| 12 | Dark mode | Neubrutalism translates but doubles visual QA | v2 |
130	| 13 | A single user being both creator and lister | One role per account — creator-listers make two accounts | v2 |
131	| 14 | Application/submission expiration timers | Kept the pipeline open indefinitely — listers close manually | v1.1 if pipelines clog |
132	
133	---
134	
135	## 4. Key Technical Decisions
136	
137	Detail lives in `tech-architecture.md` §1 (design decisions) and §3 (Apify integration).
138	
139	| Area | Decision |
140	|---|---|
141	| **Metric source** | Apify scrapers: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` (1 run per TikTok refresh) and `apify/instagram-scraper` (2 runs per IG refresh: `resultsType=details` + `resultsType=posts`). No OAuth, no Business/Creator requirement. |
142	| **Versioned fields** | `price`, `currency`, `max_submissions`, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos. Editing any of these bumps a version and cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to pending applications. |
143	| **Role cardinality** | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
144	| **Revision flow** | Binary approve/reject. No "request changes" in v1; column reserved for v1.1. |
145	| **Auto-expiration** | None. Applications and submissions stay in their inbox until acted on. Listings close only when the lister closes them. |
146	| **Submission re-use** | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` ("also submitted to N other campaigns") but never blocked. |
147	| **Eligibility re-check** | Re-evaluated at lister approve-time, not at submission-time. Prevents metric-gaming; approval locks the creator in at that moment's numbers. Lister sees `OverrideEligibilityDialog` if the applicant drifted out of eligibility between apply and approve. |
148	
149	**Supporting architectural calls:**
150	
151	- Denormalized creator metrics on `creator_profiles` (`tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_avg_views_last_10`, etc.) — populated by a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on `metric_snapshots`, scoped by `scrape_mode`, serialized with `pg_advisory_xact_lock`.
152	- Trigger-maintained `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` cache columns, btree-indexed for O(log n) feed filtering. Plain `integer` columns (not `GENERATED ALWAYS AS`) because the source lives in another table.
153	- `metric_snapshots.scrape_mode` enum (`tiktok_profile` | `ig_details` | `ig_posts`) — lets a single Apify webhook resolve into the correct subset of denormalized fields. IG's two runs land independently and can partially succeed.
154	- Manual refresh throttle: 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)` — `429` returned from edge function before any Apify call.
155	
156	---
157	
158	## 5. Design System Summary
159	
160	Full details in `docs/design.md`. Highlights:
161	
162	- **Style:** Neubrutalism (hard shadows, 2px ink borders, 12px card radius, 999px pill radius).
163	- **Palette:** Pink primary `#EC4899`, Orange CTA `#F97316`, warm canvas `#FFF9F2`, four semantic status colors with soft-bg variants.
164	- **Typography:** Clash Display (display/heading), Satoshi (body/caption), JetBrains Mono (URLs/handles).
165	- **Motion:** Reanimated 3 worklets. Three spring presets (soft/snappy/bouncy). Press-collapse shadows on every pressable. Celebration burst on approval. Shake on rejection. `useReducedMotion` respected everywhere.
166	- **Icons:** Lucide React Native only. No emoji.
167	- **Tabs:** 4 per role. Creator = Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile. Lister = Home / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile. Lister has a FAB for create-campaign.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## 6. Build Sequence (12 steps)
172	
173	Each step ends with something demonstrable. Numbered for checkpointing.
174	
175	1. **Supabase project + migration.** Apply the full schema from `tech-architecture.md` §4.7 (including `scrape_mode` enum, `metric_snapshots` v2 shape, denorm trigger). Seed: one lister, one active listing, one creator with two linked handles and fake metric snapshots. Verify RLS blocks cross-user reads.
176	2. **Custom JWT edge function + `/auth/signup-lister`.** Listers can create accounts and receive a JWT. Round-trip tested with curl. JWT claims validated by RLS on a shared read.
177	3. **Apify client wrapper + webhook receiver.** Edge function `apify-core` exposes `.runTikTokProfile(handle)`, `.runInstagramDetails(handle)`, `.runInstagramPosts(handle)`. `POST /webhooks/apify` verifies the shared secret, fetches the dataset, upserts `metric_snapshots` by `apify_run_id`, branches on `scrape_mode`. Demonstrate against live Apify sandbox with two test handles.
178	4. **`/auth/signup-creator` + initial scrape.** Handle-based signup inserts `users` + `creator_profiles` + `social_links` rows, then kicks the scraper runs synchronously (60s wait) and falls back to webhook completion. Demonstrate via curl: signup returns JWT; within 60s, `creator_profiles` has populated metrics; Supabase Studio shows the `metric_snapshots` rows.
179	5. **Metric refresh plumbing.** `POST /metrics/refresh` with the 6h throttle + `pg_cron` daily job. Manual pull works; throttle returns `429`. Cron populates stale profiles.
180	6. **Design tokens + primitive component library.** Colors, typography, spacing, `ButtonPrimary`, `ButtonSecondary`, `Chip`, `StatusPill`, `CampaignCard`, `SkeletonCard`, `BottomSheet`, `Toast`. Each with a Storybook-style preview screen.
181	7. **Creator feed + eligibility filter.** Direct PostgREST query with generated-column filters. Client-side fine-grained eligibility. Pull-to-refresh.
182	8. **Campaign detail + apply flow.** `GET /listings/:id` edge function returns listing + conditions + live eligibility. Apply modal → `POST /applications` with server re-check. Toast on success.
183	9. **Create-campaign wizard (lister).** 5 steps → `POST /listings`. Draft + publish. Review step shows creator-side preview.
184	10. **Applications inbox + review (lister) + My Applications (creator).** Lister approves/rejects. Realtime updates on both sides. Eligibility re-check on approve with override dialog.
185	11. **Submission composer + review.** Creator pastes URL → oEmbed validation → submit. Lister embeds video (WebView), checks post-conditions, approves/rejects. Status change animations wired.
186	12. **Notifications + polishing pass.** In-app inbox, bell badge, deep-link routing, listing-edit cascade UX (*"cancel 12 apps?"* modal), empty states, error states, reduced-motion audit.
187	
188	**Target velocity:** a solo dev with this stack can land steps 1–4 in week 1, 5–7 in week 2, 8–10 in week 3, 11–12 in week 4. Four-week MVP is realistic.
189	
190	---
191	
192	## 7. Reference Artifacts
193	
194	- **`docs/design.md`** — full UI/UX language, screen catalog, wireframes, component inventory, motion spec.
195	- **`docs/tech-architecture.md`** — design decisions, state machines, Apify integration specs (actor catalog, input/output schemas, webhook wiring, cost management), Supabase schema + RLS + triggers + cron, edge-function spec sheet, eligibility engine, sequence diagrams, v1 scope cuts.
196	- **`docs/product-plan.md`** (this file) — synthesis + decisions + build sequence.
197	
198	All three are living docs. Pivots that change a §4 decision (e.g. handle verification, revision-request, push notifications) must be reflected in all three files — not silently in one.
199

1	# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
53	- Body text (`--ink` `#0F172A`): **17.8:1** — passes WCAG AAA.
54	- Secondary text (`--ink-70` `#475569`): **7.9:1** — passes AAA.
55	- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
56	- CTA orange (`--cta` `#F97316`) is a background color under black label text (17.8:1), so the button itself is AAA; the orange is never used as foreground text.
57	
58	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
59	
60	### 1.3 Font Pairing
61	
62	**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
63	
64	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
67	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
68	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73	
74	**Usage rules:**
75	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
76	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
77	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78	- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79	
80	**Dynamic Type / system font scale.** Honor iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scale up to **130%** on all text — all sizes above scale proportionally via `allowFontScaling={true}` (RN default). **Cap at 130%** to protect Neubrutalism's fixed line-heights (1.15 display) and the 340px content-width cap, both of which break visually past that threshold (letters start touching; cards overflow gutters). Above 130% we clamp the scale factor in a top-level `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3` setting — the OS-level preference is still reflected up to the cap, just not beyond it. For users who need more, the v2 accessibility settings screen will expose a "Full size-up (may break layout)" escape hatch. Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed — they're UI chrome, not content.
81	
82	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83	
84	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85	```
86	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89	```
90	
91	**Timing presets:**
92	```
93	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
94	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
95	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
96	```
97	
98	**Motion rules:**
99	1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
101	3. **Status change (pending → approved/rejected)** — StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, ±4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. **Deep-link arrivals** (user tapped the push notification and the Submission Detail screen is the first screen they land on) — the celebration anchor is the StatusPill inside the just-mounted screen, triggered 220ms after mount (after the screen-slide settles) so the burst reads as a response to the arrival, not a hanging-over-from-last-screen artifact. If the screen mounts with `status` already `approved` (i.e. the user arrived via deep link, not via an in-app realtime update), we still fire the celebration exactly once, gated by a `celebration_seen_for_submission_id` entry in MMKV so revisits don't re-trigger.
102	4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
103	5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
104	6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
105	7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
106	8. **FlatList entries** — on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 → 0, opacity 0 → 1). Skip for subsequent renders. **Skip on screen re-entry within the same session** — a `useFocusEffect`-tracked `hasMountedOnce` ref gates the stagger so re-entering Feed from a pushed detail screen (or switching tabs back) doesn't replay the animation. Pull-to-refresh does *not* re-stagger either; new rows fade in individually (`LinearTransition` via `react-native-reanimated`'s layout animation API, 180ms). Full app relaunches reset the ref — the stagger is a first-impression-per-session flourish, not a per-mount one.
107	
108	**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.
109	
110	**Reduced motion:** wrap spring helpers in `useReducedMotion()`. Replace springs with `withTiming(value, { duration: 0 })` or short 120ms fade. Confetti and shake become no-ops; color transitions remain.
111	
112	### 1.5 Component Personality
113	
114	| Attribute | Decision |
115	|---|---|
116	| Corners | Cards 12px, buttons 10px, pills 999px, inputs 10px, images 8px. |
117	| Borders | 2px solid `--ink` on every card, button, input, chip. |
118	| Shadow | `offset (3, 3), color: --ink, opacity: 1, radius: 0` — signature hard shadow. Pressed collapses to (0,0). |
119	| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |
120	| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
121	| Imagery | Campaign sample videos get 2px ink border + hard shadow. Thumbnail aspect 9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for avatars. |
122	| Empty-state illustrations | Geometric line drawings using primary/cta/ink only. No stock illustration libraries. |
123	| Haptics | `expo-haptics` — Light on press, Medium on status change, Success/Error on approve/reject arrivals. |

230	**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231	
232	- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).

253	### 3.4 Deep Link Strategy
254	
255	Scheme: `marketify://`
256	
257	| Event | Deep link | Lands on | Back-stack seed |
258	|---|---|---|---|
259	| Creator app approved | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail with animated status change | Applied tab → Application Detail |
260	| Creator app rejected | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail, rejection reason expanded | Applied tab → Application Detail |
261	| Creator app cancelled (listing edited) | `marketify://applications/:id?reason=listing_edited` | Application Detail with "What changed" diff card | Applied tab → Application Detail |
262	| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab → Submission Detail |
263	| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264	| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
265	| Metric refresh ready | `marketify://profile` | Profile with refresh banner | Profile tab (own root) |
266	
267	Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.

275	### 4.0 Onboarding — Fetching Metrics (Creator)
276	
277	```
278	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
279	│                                           │
280	│                  ▓▓▓▓▓                    │ animated geometric
281	│                 ▓     ▓                   │ line illustration
282	│                 ▓     ▓                   │ (primary + ink)
283	│                  ▓▓▓▓▓                    │
284	│                                           │
285	│         Fetching your stats               │ Clash Display 22
286	│                                           │
287	│   We're pulling your public profile(s).   │ Satoshi 15
288	│   Usually takes 15–60 seconds.            │
289	│                                           │
290	│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │
291	│   │ ● TikTok       @sarahfilms   ✓ 18s │ │ per-run status row
292	│   │ ● Instagram    @sarahfilms   …     │ │ (ig_details)
293	│   │ ● Instagram — videos         …     │ │ (ig_posts)
294	│   └─────────────────────────────────────┘ │
295	│                                           │
296	│   [ Keep me here ]  [ Continue in bg → ]  │ Secondary / Primary
297	│                                           │
298	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
299	```
300	
301	**Core behavior.** After the creator submits handles on `/onboarding/creator-handles`, the edge function dispatches up to three Apify runs in parallel (1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram) and routes the creator to this screen. Each run gets a status row. The screen subscribes via realtime to `metric_snapshots` for this user.
302	
303	**Per-run row states** (reuses `MetricStaleIndicator`):
304	
305	| State | Row visual |
306	|---|---|
307	| `dispatching` | `●` (ink), handle, trailing "queued…" |
308	| `refreshing` | `●` (warning), handle, trailing animated pulsing `…` |
309	| `fresh` | `●` (success), handle, trailing "✓ 18s" (elapsed time) |
310	| `failed` | `●` (danger), handle, trailing "✗ Couldn't fetch" + inline "Retry" link (respects 6h throttle) |
311	
312	**Completion rules** (screen auto-advances based on these):
313	- **All three fresh** → 220ms soft fade → push `/(creator)/feed`. Success toast: "You're in. N campaigns match you."
314	- **All fresh except one photo-only IG `ig_posts` returned 0 videos** (not a scrape error — a real "this creator has no videos" signal, distinguishable in the webhook's `raw_payload`) → same as above; the creator enters the feed with `instagram_avg_views_last_10 = null` and only sees campaigns that don't require that metric. Success toast: "You're in. Video-views stats were skipped — you have no IG videos."
315	- **At least one `failed`** → do NOT auto-advance. The "Continue in bg" CTA upgrades to "Skip for now — you can add this later" and routes to the feed anyway; the failed handle's profile page later shows a retry card (§5.4). Success criterion is "at least one scrape landed" — if *all three* fail, the screen flips to a full-error state (below).
316	- **"Continue in background"** at any time → immediate route to the feed; outstanding runs complete in the background and update Profile metrics silently. A small `MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'` chip appears in the Profile tab's icon dot until all runs land.
317	
318	**"Keep me here"** anchors the screen; useful for impatient first-timers who want to watch the rows flip. No timeout — if the user stays, they stay.
319	
320	**Full-error state** (all three scrapes failed):
321	```
322	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
323	│                                           │
324	│              ⚠ (danger-soft)              │
325	│                                           │
326	│      Couldn't fetch any of your profiles  │
327	│                                           │
328	│   This usually means one of:              │
329	│   • The handle has a typo                 │
330	│   • The profile is private                │
331	│   • Our scraper is temporarily down       │
332	│                                           │
333	│   [ Edit handles ]    [ Skip for now ]    │
334	│                                           │
335	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
336	```
337	"Edit handles" returns to `/onboarding/creator-handles` with the values pre-filled. "Skip for now" routes to the feed; the profile shows a persistent "Add or fix a handle to see campaigns" card (they can browse but Apply is gated on at least one `fresh` platform metric set).
338	
339	**Timeouts + edge cases.**
340	- If any run is still `refreshing` after 90s, the row's trailing copy changes from animated `…` to "still working…" (same state, different reassurance). The screen does *not* surface the 10-min stuck-timeout (§3 `fail-stuck-refreshing` cron) — from the creator's POV the run either completes or they hit "Continue in bg" first.
341	- If the app is backgrounded during this screen, realtime subscriptions reconnect on foreground and the rows render the current persisted state — no stuck "refreshing" if a webhook actually landed while backgrounded.

431	**Sheet states** (internal state machine, not separate screens):
432	
433	| State | Visual | Transition |
434	|---|---|---|
435	| `input` | normal sheet, "Add handle" button enabled once @handle validates client-side | `POST /social-links` returns 201 → `dispatching` |
436	| `dispatching` | "Add handle" becomes a `ButtonPrimary loading` with an inline spinner; input is read-only | edge function enqueues Apify runs → `scraping` (usually <300ms) |
437	| `scraping` | sheet collapses to a pinned bottom progress banner (`MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'`, copy: "Fetching @sarahfilms …") and Campaign Detail becomes interactive again; eligibility rows that depend on IG enter a `refreshing` visual | one or both IG webhooks land (`ig_details` / `ig_posts`) |
438	| `partial` (IG only) | banner shows "@sarahfilms · followers ✓, views loading…"; eligibility rows that depend on `instagram_follower_count` can re-evaluate and pass / fail optimistically, while `instagram_avg_views_last_10`-dependent rows stay in `refreshing` | second webhook lands → `fresh` or `failed` |
439	| `fresh` | banner flips to a 2.5s success toast ("@sarahfilms added — you're now eligible" / "…still missing conditions"); eligibility rows re-render with final pass/fail | banner auto-dismisses; CTA re-evaluates; if now eligible the Apply CTA pulses once (`bouncy` spring) |
440	| `failed` | banner flips to `failed` MetricStaleIndicator variant with retry CTA: "Couldn't fetch @sarahfilms · Edit handle" | tap → re-opens AddHandleSheet in `input` state with handle pre-filled (doesn't unlink) |
441	| `scrape_error + handle_invalid` (distinct subkind — resolved from webhook's `error_message`) | banner copy: "@sarahfilms doesn't look like a real Instagram account. Double-check the spelling." | same as `failed` |
442	
443	**Interaction with §5.6 "Is this you?" card.** If the creator adds a handle here and immediately navigates to Profile (e.g., from the "Edit Handles" secondary action later in the session), the first-scrape confirmation card renders on Profile *once the first `fresh` snapshot exists*. The two flows are independent — AddHandleSheet is about adding the handle for eligibility in context; the confirmation card is a profile-level sanity check. A creator who never visits Profile still sees the card the first time they do.
444	
445	**What the eligibility rows show during `scraping` / `partial`.**
446	- Rows bound to the being-scraped platform render `MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'` over their user_value (e.g., "12.3K" becomes a skeleton).
447	- The Apply CTA is disabled with copy: "Waiting for @sarahfilms…" — not "Not eligible" (honesty: eligibility is currently unknown, not failed).
448	- If the creator backgrounds the app and returns, the banner + refreshing state persist (driven by realtime subscription to `metric_snapshots` for the creator's own social_links).
449	- If the creator applies to a *different* campaign in the meantime, the banner follows them on that Campaign Detail too — it's a global pinned-bottom banner, not screen-local, until all outstanding runs resolve.
450	
451	**Backend contract.** The sheet calls `POST /social-links { platform, handle }` which (a) inserts `social_links` with `status='linked'`, (b) enqueues the scrape_modes for that platform via Apify client, (c) returns `{ social_link_id, queued_scrape_modes: [...] }` synchronously. The sheet then subscribes to realtime on `metric_snapshots` filtered to `social_link_id` and drives its state machine from webhook arrivals. The sheet is the only UI that interleaves a Campaign Detail eligibility check with an active scrape; the state model above is its spec.

465	│ │ ✓ 10K+ TikTok followers            │    │

477	│ the campaign rules.                       │

480	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
481	```
482	
483	### 4.4 Submission Composer (Creator)
484	
485	```
486	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
487	│ ← Submit your video                       │
488	│ For: Clean Beauty Review                  │
489	│                                           │
490	│ ══════ Platform ══════                    │
491	│ [ TikTok ] [ Instagram ]                  │ ← Segmented control
492	│                                           │
493	│ ══════ Paste your video URL ══════        │
494	│ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐    │
495	│ │ https://tiktok.com/@me/video/…     │    │
496	│ └────────────────────────────────────┘    │
497	│ ⓘ We'll validate the link.                │
498	│                                           │
499	│ ══════ Preview ══════                     │
500	│ ┌────────────────────┐                    │
501	│ │   [thumbnail]      │                    │
502	│ └────────────────────┘                    │
503	│                                           │
504	│ ══════ Rules checklist (self-affirm) ═══  │
505	│ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐    │
506	│ │ ☐ 30s vertical video               │    │
507	│ │ ☐ Mentions "cruelty-free"          │    │
508	│ │ ☐ Family-friendly                  │    │
509	│ │ ☐ Link in bio to cleanco.com       │    │
510	│ └────────────────────────────────────┘    │
511	│                                           │
512	│ [    SEND FOR REVIEW    ]                 │
513	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
514	```
515	
516	**States:** Empty URL (dashed placeholder), Invalid (danger border + helper), Fetching (shimmer), Preview failed (non-blocking), Submitting (spinner), Success (full-screen card, auto-dismiss).
517	
518	### 4.5 Create Campaign Wizard (Lister)
519	
520	```
521	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
522	│ ← Step 3 of 5                             │
523	│ ●●●○○                                     │
524	│ Who can apply?                            │
525	│ Set eligibility rules for creators.       │
526	│                                           │
527	│ ══════ TikTok ══════                      │
528	│ Min followers                             │
529	│ ┌──────────────┐                          │
530	│ │ 10,000       │                          │
531	│ └──────────────┘                          │
532	│ Min avg views                             │
533	│ ┌──────────────┐                          │
534	│ │ 5,000        │                          │
535	│ └──────────────┘                          │
536	│                                           │
537	│ ══════ Instagram ══════                   │
538	│ Min followers  ...                        │
539	│                                           │
540	│ [+ Required]  ← both platforms?           │
541	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
542	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
543	│                           [ NEXT → ]      │
544	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
545	```
546	
547	Step-by-step: Basics → Price → Pre-conditions → Post-conditions → Samples → Review.
548	
549	### 4.6 Submission Review (Lister)
550	
551	```
552	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
553	│ ← Review submission                       │
554	│ From: @sarahfilms                         │
555	│ For: Clean Beauty Review                  │
556	│ ┌────────────────────────────────┐        │ ReuseBadge — header variant
557	│ │ ↻ Also submitted to 2 others   │        │ tap → sheet listing the other campaigns
558	│ └────────────────────────────────┘        │ (warning-soft bg, not blocking)
559	│                                           │
560	│ ══════ Video ══════                       │
561	│ ┌──────────────────────┐                  │
562	│ │   [video preview]  ▶ │                  │
563	│ └──────────────────────┘                  │
564	│ tiktok.com/@sarahfilms/video/123          │
565	│                                           │
566	│ ══════ Check the rules ══════             │
567	│ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐    │ 3-state checkboxes
568	│ │ ☐ 30s vertical video               │    │
569	│ │ ✓ Mentions "cruelty-free"          │    │
570	│ │ ☐ Family-friendly                  │    │
571	│ │ ✗ Link in bio to cleanco.com       │    │ ← failed row stays red
572	│ └────────────────────────────────────┘    │
573	│                                           │
574	│ ══════ Feedback (optional) ══════         │
575	│ ┌────────────────────────────────────┐    │
576	│ │ Message to creator…        0/240   │    │
577	│ └────────────────────────────────────┘    │
578	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
579	┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
580	│ [   REJECT   ] [    APPROVE ✓    ]        │
581	└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
582	
583	If APPROVE tapped with any ✗ rows → OverrideEligibilityDialog:
584	
585	┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
586	│  Approve anyway?                       │
587	│                                        │
588	│  1 rule wasn't met:                    │
589	│  • Link in bio to cleanco.com          │
590	│                                        │
591	│  Approving overrides this. The creator │
592	│  will be paid in full.                 │
593	│                                        │
594	│  Type OVERRIDE to confirm:             │
595	│  ┌──────────────────────────────────┐  │
596	│  │                                  │  │
597	│  └──────────────────────────────────┘  │
598	│                                        │
599	│  [ Cancel ]   [ Approve with override ]│
600	└────────────────────────────────────────┘
601	```
602	
603	Notes on the two Q-driven affordances:
604	- **ReuseBadge** reads from the `submission_reuse_view` server aggregate; tap opens a bottom sheet showing the other campaigns with their status so the lister can decide whether reuse is a problem for *their* brief (some briefs permit cross-posting, others require exclusivity).
605	- **OverrideEligibilityDialog** only appears when approving with failed post-conditions. Typed confirmation (not just a button) is intentional friction — overrides are auditable (`submissions.override_reason`, `submissions.override_by_user_id`) and we want the lister to pause. Reject-with-failed-conditions needs no dialog (expected path).
606	
607	**Self-affirm vs review checkbox mapping.** The Submission Composer (creator, §4.4) and Submission Review (lister, §4.6) render the same `ConditionChecklist` with different `mode` props:
608	- `mode='self'` (composer) → two-state: `☐` unchecked / `☑` checked. Every row starts unchecked; the creator affirms each one. No `✗` red state — a creator who can't honestly affirm a row just leaves it unchecked, and the client-side validator blocks submit until all rows are checked.
609	- `mode='review'` (lister) → three-state: `☐` unreviewed / `✓` passes / `✗` fails. Rows land in unreviewed; the lister taps to cycle through the three states. `✗` rows persist their red visual until decision, and light up the OverrideEligibilityDialog trigger on Approve.
610	
611	---
612	
613	## 5. Critical UX Moments
614	
615	### 5.1 Eligibility Failure (Soft Rejection)
616	
617	**Tone:** Never "you're not good enough." Always "here's what's missing, here's how to fix it."
618	
619	- **Copy:** "You're not eligible yet for this one."
620	- **Visual:** danger-soft background (warm, not aggressive red); danger icon (subtle circle-slash, not harsh X).
621	- **Always pair with action:** inline CTA per failing row ("Link Instagram", "Refresh metrics").
622	- **Motion:** failing rows fade in *after* passing rows (200ms delay), gentle single-pulse danger-soft background. No shaking.
623	- **Escape hatch:** "Notify me when I qualify" toggle at the bottom.
624	
625	### 5.2 Application Auto-Cancellation (Lister Edited Listing)
626	
627	This is a trust-critical moment.
628	
629	- **Notification copy:** "@cleanco updated Clean Beauty Review. Your application was cancelled — you can re-apply with the new terms."
630	- **Deep link:** Application Detail with a distinctive **"What changed" diff card** at top (cancelled-soft bg, cancelled-color 2px border, no shadow).
631	- **Diff card:** side-by-side old vs new for changed fields (price, pre/post-conditions). Changed values in primary-soft pills.
632	- **Primary CTA:** "Re-apply" (cta color) — takes creator to new campaign detail pre-scrolled to eligibility.
633	- **Secondary:** "Dismiss".
634	- **Motion:** status pill transitions pending/approved → cancelled with 600ms color wash (no bounce — neutral).
635	- **Lister side:** "This will cancel **N** pending applications. They'll be notified." Requires explicit confirmation button.
636	- **Lister-side warning on subsequent edits:** after the first version bump, every subsequent edit confirmation also surfaces: *"Creators cancelled by a previous edit are **not** re-notified. Only currently active applications (N) will be told about this change."* This is a one-way fan-out — by design, to prevent spamming ex-applicants — but listers have expected "edit again = re-ping everyone" and been surprised. Copy is inline in the ConfirmationModal body, not a separate dialog, so it reads as context, not another obstacle.
637	
638	### 5.3 Submission Status Change (pending → approved/rejected)
639	
640	The most emotional moment.
641	
642	**Approved:**
643	- Deep-link to Submission Detail.
644	- StatusPill: 200ms warning → 320ms color-interpolated success + bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1).
645	- Confetti-lite: 8 small 6×6 squares burst upward, fade over 800ms.
646	- Haptic: `Success`.
647	- Banner: "Approved — nice work!" cta-colored, auto-dismiss 2.5s.
648	- All respects `useReducedMotion`.
649	
650	**Rejected:**
651	- Same deep-link.
652	- StatusPill: 400ms warning → danger wash + **one shake gesture made of 3 oscillation cycles** (±4px each, 120ms per half-cycle, ~720ms total). No confetti.
653	- Haptic: `Warning` (not Error — not punitive).
654	- Lister feedback card always visible (danger-soft bg).
655	- **Primary CTA: "Find a similar bounty"** — routes to Feed pre-filtered by the rejected campaign's `listings.category` + a price band of ±30% around its `listings.price_cents`. Both columns exist in the schema (see `tech-architecture.md` §7); the feed uses the `listings_category_price_idx` partial index for sub-ms response. The rejected path must offer forward motion, not just a closed door.
656	- **Secondary CTA: "Read campaign rules"** — re-orients toward learning for anyone who wants to understand the rejection.
657	- Copy under the feedback card: *"This one didn't land. Plenty more that fit your profile."* Blameless, forward-looking.
658	- Silent landing — no banner, no confetti anti-pattern.
659	
660	### 5.4 Metrics Refresh
661	
662	**State model is per-metric, not per-platform.** A single Instagram refresh is two independent Apify runs (follower count + avg views). They can land out-of-order, and one can fail while the other succeeds. TikTok is a single run and collapses to the simple case.
663	
664	**Per-metric states** (rendered by `MetricStaleIndicator`):
665	
666	| State | Visual | When |
667	|---|---|---|
668	| `fresh` | no chip; timestamp reads "Updated 2m ago" | last snapshot for this `scrape_mode` is `fresh` and `< 24h` old |
669	| `stale` | tiny warning-soft dot; timestamp reads "Updated 3d ago" | `fresh` but `> 24h` (auto-flipped by cron) |
670	| `refreshing` | faint pulsing skeleton over the metric value | a webhook is outstanding |
671	| `failed` | danger-soft chip: "Couldn't refresh · Retry" | last snapshot is `failed`; tapping retries (respects 6h throttle) |
672	
673	**Pull-to-refresh gesture** on Profile:
674	- **Pull phase:** counter above metric tiles interpolates upward; "Release to refresh".
675	- **Release phase:** all per-metric tiles owned by the platform(s) being refreshed enter `refreshing` simultaneously. Per-platform odometer only starts when that mode's webhook lands.
676	- **Partial-success phase (IG):** if `ig_details` lands first, follower count runs the odometer + delta pill; `ig_avg_views_last_10` stays in `refreshing` with its skeleton until `ig_posts` lands. If `ig_posts` fails (e.g. photo-only profile), the views tile flips to `failed` with "Not enough video content" as the specific copy (we distinguish "no videos" from "scrape error" by checking `raw_payload`).
677	- **Full-success phase:** each mode's tile runs its own odometer with bouncy spring and fades its delta pill (`+1.2K`/`-300`) for 1.5s.
678	- **Error phase:** if a manual refresh hits the 6h throttle (`429 RATE_LIMIT`), toast surfaces the exact `retry_after_sec` using a **tiered format**:
679	  - `≥ 1h`: `"Try again in Xh Ym."` (e.g. "Try again in 4h 23m.")
680	  - `60s ≤ t < 1h`: `"Try again in Xm."` (e.g. "Try again in 7m.")
681	  - `< 60s`: `"Try again in a few seconds."` (we round down aggressively here — nobody benefits from "try again in 11s")
682	  Never "in a minute" unless `retry_after_sec` literally falls between 60 and 119.
683	- **Last-refreshed timestamp** is per-platform, not per-metric, and reads the **older** of the two IG snapshots: "IG updated 14m ago" (not "5m ago" — honesty over recency).
684	
685	### 5.5 Empty State — No Eligible Campaigns
686	
687	- **Illustration:** geometric line art (primary + ink only).
688	- **Headline:** "Nothing matches you — yet."
689	- **Body:** "Campaigns get added daily. Meanwhile, here's what to try."
690	- **Two action cards:**
691	  - **"Refresh your metrics"** → Profile, auto-triggers refresh.
692	  - **"See all campaigns"** → flips eligibility toggle off, reloads feed.
693	- **Never** just "no results".
694	
695	### 5.6 First-Scrape Handle Confirmation ("Is this you?")
696	
697	Handles are self-declared — no OAuth, no ownership verification. That's fine for v1, but we want one *soft* confirmation step the first time a handle resolves to a real profile, so the creator catches their own typos (`@sarah.films` vs `@sarahfilms`) before we lean on the metrics for eligibility.
698	
699	- **When:** on the Profile screen, the first time *any* scrape for a newly-added handle lands with `status='fresh'` (not on re-scrapes, not on re-linked handles). Gated by `handle_confirmed_at` on `social_links`.
700	- **What renders:** an inline confirmation card at the top of Profile, *above* the metric tiles, with the scraped display name + avatar + the bio-first-line, and two buttons:
701	  - **"Yes, that's me"** → sets `handle_confirmed_at = now()`, card fades out (220ms short).
702	  - **"Wrong profile"** → opens Edit Handles with the handle pre-selected in the edit row. Doesn't un-link automatically — the creator re-types.
703	- **Tone:** neutral, not alarmed. Copy: *"Quick check — is this your account? We'll use these stats for campaign eligibility."*
704	- **Visual:** primary-soft background card, 2px ink border, hard shadow. Avatar is 1:1 with 2px border.
705	- **No blocking:** metric tiles render normally below the card. The creator can ignore it — the card just doesn't go away until they answer. Applications still work; the card is a nudge, not a gate.
706	- **Unconfirmed-handle footgun:** if a creator ignores this and we later detect their handle resolves to a suspiciously-different profile (e.g., metrics drop 90%+ between refreshes, bio changes language), we re-surface the card with danger-soft styling and slightly firmer copy — but this is a v2 concern; MVP just shows the card once per handle.
707	
708	---
709	
710	## 6. Component Library Inventory
711	
712	| Component | Purpose | Key props |
713	|---|---|---|
714	| **CampaignCard** | Feed + inbox cell | `title, company, platforms[], price, eligibility, variant: 'creator'\|'lister', counts?` |
715	| **StatusPill** | Status indicator | `status`, exposes `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet |
716	| **EligibilityBadge** | Compact badge on card | `eligible: boolean, reason?` |
717	| **EligibilityRow** | Single pre-condition row | `label, required, user_value, passed, action?` |
718	| **ConditionChecklist** | Post-condition list | `items[], mode: 'self'\|'review', onChange` |
719	| **MetricChip** | Follower / avg-views chip | `platform, label, value, delta?, lastUpdated?` |
720	| **VideoURLInput** | TikTok/IG URL with preview | `platform, onChange, onPreview` |
721	| **VideoThumbnail** | 9:16 or 1:1 preview | `url, aspect, onPress` |
722	| **ButtonPrimary** | CTA button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled, variant` |
723	| **ButtonSecondary** | Low-emphasis button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled` |
724	| **FAB** | Floating action (Lister) | `icon, onPress` |
725	| **Chip** | Filter + category | `label, active, variant, onPress` |
726	| **SectionHeader** | ══════ Label ══════ | `title, action?` |
727	| **ProgressDots** | Wizard progress | `total, current` |
728	| **DiffCard** | "What changed" card | `oldValue, newValue, fieldName` |
729	| **EmptyState** | Generic empty | `illustration: EmptyStateIllustrationKey, title, body, primaryAction, secondaryAction?` — `EmptyStateIllustrationKey` is a string-keyed registry: `'no_campaigns' \| 'no_eligible' \| 'no_applications' \| 'no_submissions' \| 'lister_no_campaigns' \| 'lister_no_applications' \| 'lister_no_submissions' \| 'network_error' \| 'not_found'`. Each key maps to a ship-in-repo SVG (`assets/illustrations/<key>.svg`) using only `--primary` / `--cta` / `--ink` per §1.5 |
730	| **ErrorState** | Recoverable error | `title, body, onRetry` |
731	| **SkeletonCard** | Loading placeholder | `height, shape` |
732	| **RefreshOdometer** | Number-scroll animation | `value, previousValue` |
733	| **Toast** | Top banner | `message, variant, duration` |
734	| **ConfirmationModal** | Two-button alert | `title, body, primaryLabel, onConfirm, variant` |
735	| **BottomSheet** | Filters + quick actions | `children, snapPoints` |
736	| **CelebrationBurst** | Approval confetti | `anchorRef` |
737	| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738	| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
739	| **ReuseBadge** | "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip on submission row / review sheet — surfaces cross-posting without blocking it | `count, variant: 'inline'\|'header'` |
740	| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
741	| **MetricStaleIndicator** | Per-metric tri-state marker attached to `MetricChip` (fresh / stale / refreshing / failed) — drives the visual in §5.4 | `state: 'fresh'\|'stale'\|'refreshing'\|'failed', retryAfterSec?, errorKind?: 'no_videos'\|'scrape_error', onRetry?` |
742	| **AddHandleSheet** | Bottom-sheet flow that adds a TikTok/IG handle inline from any surface (Campaign Detail eligibility row, Edit Handles). Owns its own state machine across `input → dispatching → scraping → (partial) → fresh \| failed` — see §4.2 | `platform: 'tiktok'\|'instagram', preFilledHandle?: string, onSuccess: (socialLinkId) => void, onCancel: () => void` |
743	
744	---
745	
746	## 7. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
747	
748	- Heavy skeuomorphism.
749	- Emoji as UI icons.
750	- Soft blurred drop shadows — breaks the Neubrutalism contract.
751	- Scale-transform hover states that cause layout shift.
752	- Punitive copy around eligibility, rejection, or cancellation. Always blameless.
753	- 5-tab bars.
754	- Animated celebrations on the *rejected* path.
755	- Dense color coding without icons — every status uses color + icon + text.
756	- Hiding metric refresh as a tiny setting. Pull-to-refresh is the gesture users expect.
757	- Modal-over-modal.
758	
759	---
760	
761	## 8. Pre-Delivery Checklist
762	
763	- [ ] All SVG icons sourced from Lucide React Native, consistent 24×24 viewBox
764	- [ ] No emojis used as UI elements
765	- [ ] All `Pressable` components have press feedback
766	- [ ] Press/hover transitions 150–300ms
767	- [ ] Body text contrast verified ≥ 4.5:1 (`#0F172A` on `#FFF9F2` = 17.8:1, AAA)
768	- [ ] Primary pink `#EC4899` never used as foreground text at body size (fails AAA at 3.3:1) — swap to `--primary-deep` `#831843` where text meaning is required
769	- [ ] All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`
770	- [ ] All `EmptyState` usages reference a key from the illustration registry; no loose illustration SVGs
771	- [ ] Focus states visible for external keyboard
772	- [ ] `useReducedMotion()` respected on all spring/shake/confetti
773	- [ ] Responsive across 360dp / 375pt / 390pt / 430pt
774	- [ ] Safe-area insets handled
775	- [ ] Color is never the sole status indicator
776	- [ ] All images have `accessibilityLabel`
777

content.content:
1	# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document
2	
3	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5	**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Design Decisions
10	
11	The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on — if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in §4 (schema), §5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).
12	
13	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
14	
15	| Area | Decision |
16	|---|---|
17	| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
18	| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
19	| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
20	| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
21	| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP — first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |
22	
23	### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle
24	
25	| Area | Decision |
26	|---|---|
27	| States | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. No `expired` state. |
28	| Auto-expiration | None. `end_date` column retained for v1.1; listers close listings manually. |
29	| Submission cap | `max_submissions` nullable. Listers close manually when they're done — no auto-close trigger in v1. |
30	| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |
31	| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field — price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions — bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
32	
33	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
34	
35	| Area | Decision |
36	|---|---|
37	| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` → `withdrawn`. |
38	| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
39	| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
40	| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
41	| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
42	| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design §4.6) but not blocked. |
43	| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
44	| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |
45	
46	### 1.4 Money
47	
48	| Area | Decision |
49	|---|---|
50	| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |
51	
52	### 1.5 Notifications
53	
54	| Area | Decision |
55	|---|---|
56	| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
57	| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
58	| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |
59	
60	### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
61	
62	| Area | Decision |
63	|---|---|
64	| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65	| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66	| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
67	| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
68	
69	### 1.7 Security / Abuse
70	
71	| Area | Decision |
72	|---|---|
73	| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button → admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
74	| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |

78	## 2. State Machines
79	
80	Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.
81	
82	### 2.1 Listing
83	
84	```
85	(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
86	draft ──publish[lister]──> active
87	active ──pause[lister]──> paused
88	paused ──resume[lister]──> active
89	active ──close[lister]──> closed
90	closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
91	paused ──archive[lister]──> archived
92	
93	Side-effect on edit mutating price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
94	  version++ AND cascade-cancel pending applications (see §2.3)
95	```
96	
97	Terminal: `archived`.
98	
99	### 2.2 Application
100	
101	```
102	(init) ──submit[creator, gated by eligibility]──> pending
103	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
104	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
105	pending ──withdraw[creator]──> withdrawn
106	pending ──listing_version_bump[system]──> cancelled_listing_edit
107	pending ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed
108	approved ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed
109	```
110	
111	No time-based expiration in v1.
112	
113	### 2.3 Submission
114	
115	```
116	(init, requires application.status=approved) ──create[creator]──> pending
117	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
118	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
119	```
120	
121	No `revision_requested` in v1. No time-based expiration.
122	
123	### 2.4 Social Link
124	
125	```
126	(init) ──handle_added[creator]──> linked       (triggers initial Apify scrape)
127	linked ──scrape_success[system]──> linked
128	linked ──scrape_fail x3[system]──> failed_fetch
129	failed_fetch ──handle_reentered[creator]──> linked
130	linked ──unlink[creator]──> unlinked
131	```
132	
133	### 2.5 Metric Snapshot
134	
135	```
136	(init, on link or refresh) ──apify_run_started[system]──> refreshing
137	refreshing ──success[system|apify_webhook]──> fresh
138	refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
139	fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
140	stale ──run_started[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
141	failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
142	```
143	
144	Rule: eligibility filter uses `fresh` OR `stale`, never `failed`. UI warns if `stale`.
